description: Initial assistant turn exposes the main workflow choices.
Guided CAP consult reaches a final ProbID analysis.

request.message: Fever, tachypnea, pleuritic cough, oxygen saturation 89 percent, and focal crackles.
WBC elevated and procalcitonin elevated.
Sputum culture pending and blood cultures negative so far.
Chest x ray shows a right lower lobe consolidation.

description: AST text with susceptible-dose-dependent wording stays in MechID instead of DoseID.

request.message: Please interpret this susceptibility pattern. E coli bloodstream isolate. Ceftriaxone resistant, cefepime susceptible dose dependent, piperacillin-tazobactam susceptible, meropenem susceptible, ciprofloxacin resistant.

description: Negation should preserve a benign delayed rash phenotype and keep cefazolin preferred.

request.message: Remote childhood amoxicillin rash only, no anaphylaxis, now needs cefazolin.

description: A vague allergy prompt followed by details should not crash and should stay low risk.

request.message: Can you help with antibiotic allergy compatibility? The patient says they had a penicillin allergy.
Remote childhood amoxicillin rash only, no anaphylaxis, now needs cefazolin.

description: Generic fungal treatment questions should prompt the mold-vs-candida clarification flow.

request.message: should ID start antifungal treatment for this patient with possible fungal infection?

description: Explicit invasive mold treatment consults should route into the invasive mold pathway.

request.message: should I start mold-active therapy for this neutropenic patient with pulmonary nodules and positive galactomannan?

description: DoseID should produce a hemodialysis cefepime regimen from one natural-language turn.

request.message: Can you help with cefepime dosing? 72 kg man on intermittent hemodialysis with Pseudomonas bacteremia.

description: RIPE dosing should use total body weight for pyrazinamide and ethambutol in the standard pathway.

request.message: RIPE dosing for 62 kg, CrCl 35, female, 165 cm.

description: ImmunoID should recognize rituximab plus steroids and produce screening/prophylaxis output.

request.message: Can you review immunosuppression prophylaxis? Rituximab plus prednisone 20 mg daily planned for 6 weeks. HBsAg negative, anti-HBc positive, anti-HBs negative. QuantiFERON negative.

description: Guided septic arthritis consult reaches a final ProbID analysis.

request.message: monoarthritis, fever, painful range of motion, unable to bear weight
ESR elevated, CRP elevated, synovial WBC over 50000
gram stain positive, blood cultures positive
ultrasound effusion present

description: Guided bacterial meningitis consult reaches a final ProbID analysis.

request.message: fever, headache, neck stiffness, altered mental status
CSF pleocytosis, CSF neutrophil predominance, CSF protein elevated, CSF glucose low
CSF gram stain positive, blood cultures positive
CT not done

description: Guided encephalitis consult reaches a final ProbID analysis.

request.message: fever, altered mental status, behavioral change, seizure
CSF pleocytosis, CSF lymphocytic predominance, CSF protein elevated
HSV PCR positive
MRI temporal lobe positive

description: Guided spinal epidural abscess consult reaches a final ProbID analysis.

request.message: back pain, fever, spinal tenderness, neurologic deficit
ESR elevated, CRP elevated
blood cultures positive
MRI positive

description: Guided brain abscess consult reaches a final ProbID analysis.

request.message: headache, fever, focal deficit, seizure
CRP elevated, WBC elevated
blood cultures positive
MRI with diffusion restriction

description: Guided necrotizing soft tissue infection consult reaches a final ProbID analysis.

request.message: pain out of proportion, rapid progression, hypotension, bullae, crepitus
WBC elevated, CRP elevated, LRINEC high
blood cultures positive
CT compatible with NSTI

description: Guided tb uveitis consult reaches a final ProbID analysis.

request.message: This is an ophthalmology visit for choroidal tuberculoma in a patient from a TB endemic region.
Tuberculin skin test positive and QuantiFERON positive.
Chest x ray positive for healed or active TB signs.
